name: Technical University of Munich

other: Subject-specific aptitude test for some programs

description: Germany's top-ranked technical university and one of Europe's finest. Consistently ranked #1 in Germany for engineering and computer science. Strong research culture, excellent Munich location, outstanding alumni network including Nobel laureates and tech founders.

name: RWTH Aachen University
programs: MSc Electrical Engineering and Information Technology
description: Germany's largest technical university and a global leader in engineering education. Known for close industry partnerships and research excellence. Located near Cologne and Brussels, excellent for automotive, electronics, and manufacturing careers.

name: TU Berlin
description: Berlin's premier technical university in Germany's startup capital. Excellent location for networking and industry internships. Strong CS and AI research groups. Access to Berlin's vibrant tech ecosystem and proximity to other Berlin universities.

name: TU Braunschweig
description: Germany's oldest TU9 university, founded in 1745. Strong in automotive, aerospace, and information technology. Located in Lower Saxony, heart of German automotive research. Close partnerships with Volkswagen, Continental, and other major automotive players.

name: TU Darmstadt
description: Germany's leading IT security research university and strong in autonomous systems. Located 30 minutes from Frankfurt. The IT Security master's program is world-renowned. Excellent for students interested in cybersecurity, AI, and autonomous vehicles.

name: TU Dresden
programs: MSc Advanced Computational and Civil Engineering Structural Studies
description: Excellence Initiative university and the largest university in the eastern German state of Saxony. Strong in semiconductor research (Silicon Saxony cluster), distributed systems, and biomedical engineering. Very affordable cost of living.

name: Leibniz University Hannover
description: Named after philosopher Leibniz, this TU9 university excels in engineering and natural sciences. Hannover is home to the world's largest industrial trade fair (Hannover Messe), providing excellent industry networking opportunities.

name: Karlsruhe Institute of Technology
description: Germany's research university in the Helmholtz Association. Consistently among the top 5 German universities for CS and engineering. Close proximity to SAP headquarters and Baden-Württemberg's thriving tech corridor. Excellent for AI, robotics, and software engineering.

name: University of Stuttgart
description: TU9 member in the heartland of German engineering excellence. Surrounded by global automotive giants (Mercedes-Benz, Porsche, Bosch). Strong in robotics, simulation, and autonomous driving. Excellent industry partnerships and internship culture.

name: LMU Munich
description: Germany's second-highest ranked university and Munich's comprehensive research powerhouse. Among the top 60 globally. Exceptional research environment, Nobel Prize tradition, and strong data science programs. Located in Munich alongside TUM.

name: Heidelberg University
description: Germany's oldest university (est. 1386) and consistently among Europe's elite. Excellence Initiative university with world-class research in bioinformatics and data science. Close to SAP headquarters and surrounded by pharma and biotech companies.

name: Humboldt University of Berlin
description: One of Berlin's top universities and a global leader in humanities, sciences, and interdisciplinary research. Named after Wilhelm and Alexander von Humboldt. Located in central Berlin with access to the city's vibrant tech and startup scene.

name: Free University of Berlin
description: Excellence Initiative university and part of the Berlin University Alliance. Strong international orientation with partnerships worldwide. Computer Science and bioinformatics programs are particularly strong. Access to Berlin's tech ecosystem.

name: University of Göttingen
description: Historic Excellence Initiative university with 45+ Nobel laureates among faculty and alumni. Strong in fundamental sciences, mathematics, and interdisciplinary research. Close to Max Planck Institutes. Affordable student city with excellent research infrastructure.

name: University of Hamburg
description: Excellence Initiative university in Germany's second-largest city and Europe's second-largest port. Strong AI research cluster with the German Research Center for Artificial Intelligence (DFKI). Access to Hamburg's logistics, aviation, and media industries.

name: University of Cologne
programs: MSc Data Science and Scientific Computing
description: One of Germany's largest universities with over 50,000 students. Strong data science program and growing AI research. Located in Cologne, with easy access to Bonn and Düsseldorf tech companies. Excellent for students seeking a vibrant urban student experience.

name: University of Tübingen
description: Excellence Initiative university and global leader in machine learning research. Home to the International Max Planck Research School for Intelligent Systems. THE top destination in Germany for ML/AI students. Bernhard Schölkopf (kernel methods) is based here.

name: University of Freiburg
description: Excellence Initiative university in the Black Forest. Strong in robotics, autonomous systems, and embedded computing. Close to France and Switzerland, offering a tri-border region advantage for international careers. Beautiful city with excellent quality of life.

name: University of Bonn
description: Excellence Initiative university and former West German capital. Known for Hausdorff Center for Mathematics and strong computing research. Deutsche Telekom and DHL headquarters are in Bonn. Affordable cost of living near Cologne.

name: University of Münster
description: Excellence Initiative university known as the 'bicycle capital' of Germany. Strong in geoinformatics and information systems. Very active student life with over 50,000 students. Affordable and highly liveable city in NRW.

name: Goethe University Frankfurt
name_german: Johann Wolfgang Goethe-Universität Frankfurt am Main
description: Excellence Initiative university located in Europe's financial capital. Exceptional for finance-tech (fintech), data science in finance, and quantitative computing. Frankfurt's ECB, Deutsche Bank, and global finance sector provide unparalleled career access.

name: University of Mannheim
description: Germany's top business university adjacent to SAP's headquarters. Exceptionally strong in business informatics and data science. Located in the Rhine-Neckar tech corridor. Strong analytics programs highly sought after by SAP and consulting firms.

name: University of Bremen
description: Excellence Initiative university with strong space technology and intelligent systems programs. Home to DFKI (German Research Center for AI) campus. Airbus has major production facilities nearby. Strong international research partnerships.

name: Bielefeld University
description: Excellence Initiative university with the CITEC (Center of Excellence for Cognitive Interaction Technology) making it a European hub for intelligent systems and cognitive robotics. Very affordable city with modern campus facilities built around a central hall concept.

name: Ruhr University Bochum
programs: MSc Electrical Engineering and Information Technology
description: Excellence Initiative university in the Ruhr valley. Home to CASA (Cyber Security in the Age of Large-Scale Adversaries) – Germany's premier cybersecurity cluster. Strong engineering tradition transitioning toward digital industries. Largest university campus in Germany.

name: University of Duisburg-Essen
description: Excellence Initiative university formed from the merger of two universities. Located in the Ruhr metropolitan area with easy access to Cologne, Dortmund, and Düsseldorf. Strong data science programs and good industry connections to Rhine-Ruhr companies.

name: Saarland University
description: Excellence Initiative university and home to multiple Max Planck Institutes for informatics and software systems. Close to France with a tri-border advantage (Germany, France, Luxembourg). Saarbrücken is Europe's largest AI/CS research hub per capita.

name: Hamburg University of Technology
description: Hamburg's dedicated technical university offering focused engineering and technology programs. Close partnership with Airbus, Hamburg Port, and local tech companies. Part of the Hamburg Innovation Port ecosystem. Strong green technology research.

name: University of Ulm
description: Excellence Initiative university in Einstein's birthplace. Strong in software engineering and biomedical technology. The Ulm Science Park hosts numerous tech companies on campus. Known for the Science City concept integrating university and industry.

name: University of Augsburg
description: Excellence Initiative university close to Munich. Strong in applied computer science, mechatronics, and physics. KUKA Robotics and MAN have major research partnerships. Augsburg is Bavaria's third-largest city with lower costs than Munich but similar job access.

name: HTW Berlin – University of Applied Sciences
name_german: Hochschule für Technik und Wirtschaft Berlin
description: Berlin's largest university of applied sciences with strong practical engineering and CS programs. Excellent industry connections in Berlin's tech ecosystem. Good alternative to research universities for those wanting applied focus and good job prospects.

name: HAW Hamburg
description: Hamburg's largest applied sciences university with strong ties to Hamburg's aviation, port, and media industries. Practical, industry-focused programs. Very good employment outcomes in Hamburg's strong economy.

name: Hochschule München
programs: MSc Applied Research in Engineering Sciences
description: Munich's applied sciences university – Germany's largest Hochschule by enrollment. Exceptional industry connections with Munich's premium employers. Practical engineering and CS programs with internship culture. Excellent job outcomes despite Munich's high cost of living.

name: Hochschule Karlsruhe
description: Applied sciences university in Karlsruhe's tech hub, adjacent to KIT. Strong engineering and CS programs with industry co-operation. Located in Germany's 'technology region Karlsruhe'. Excellent for students wanting applied engineering in the SAP/Siemens corridor.

name: OTH Regensburg
programs: MSc Applied Research in Engineering Sciences
description: Applied sciences university in the historic city of Regensburg, Bavaria. Strong partnerships with BMW and automotive suppliers. Good CS and electronics programs. Regensburg has a growing tech sector and very affordable living costs compared to Munich.

name: Hochschule Furtwangen University
description: Specialized applied sciences university in the Black Forest region known for clock-making tradition and precision engineering. Strong CS and embedded systems programs. Very affordable living costs. Good practical focus with industry-linked projects.

name: Hochschule Heilbronn
description: Applied sciences university in Heilbronn, home to Germany's largest private employer (Schwarz Group – Lidl/Kaufland). Strong automotive and IT programs. The Innovation Park Artificial Intelligence (IPAI) is being built here, making it a future AI hub.

name: Hochschule Reutlingen
description: Applied sciences university near Stuttgart with strong international programs. Located in Baden-Württemberg's 'tech triangle' between Stuttgart, Tübingen, and Ulm. Good English-taught programs for international students. ESB Business School on campus is well-regarded.

name: Hochschule Pforzheim
description: Applied sciences university between Karlsruhe and Stuttgart, near Porsche's R&D facilities. Strong engineering-management hybrid programs. Good for students wanting to combine technical and business skills. Access to Baden-Württemberg's automotive corridor.

name: Hochschule Esslingen
name_german: Hochschule Esslingen – University of Applied Sciences
description: Applied sciences university directly adjacent to Stuttgart and Daimler's headquarters. Excellent automotive and embedded systems programs with direct company partnerships. Germany's oldest technical higher education institution. Very high practical relevance.

name: FH Aachen University of Applied Sciences
description: Applied sciences university in the same city as RWTH Aachen. More accessible admission requirements but strong practical engineering programs. Aerospace engineering program is among Germany's best at applied level. Good proximity to Belgium and Netherlands.

name: Hochschule Darmstadt
name_german: Hochschule Darmstadt – University of Applied Sciences
description: Applied sciences university in Darmstadt's 'Science City'. Located near TU Darmstadt and Software AG headquarters. Strong IT security and distributed systems programs. Access to Darmstadt's dense IT and software cluster.

name: Hochschule Düsseldorf
name_german: Hochschule Düsseldorf – University of Applied Sciences
description: Applied sciences university in Germany's fashion and trade capital. Located in NRW's economic center. Strong biomedical engineering program and IT programs. Düsseldorf's international business environment provides diverse career opportunities.

name: Hochschule Osnabrück
name_german: Hochschule Osnabrück – University of Applied Sciences
description: Applied sciences university in Lower Saxony with strong agricultural tech and mechanical engineering. Good for students interested in food tech, agricultural informatics, or general engineering. Affordable small-city living with good industry access.

name: Hochschule Niederrhein
name_german: Hochschule Niederrhein – University of Applied Sciences
programs: MSc Computer Science and Information Technology
description: Applied sciences university in the lower Rhine region with campuses in Krefeld and Mönchengladbach. Strong in textile technology, chemistry, and engineering informatics. Close to Düsseldorf and within NRW's industrial corridor. Practical focus with small class sizes.

name: Hochschule Mannheim
name_german: Hochschule Mannheim – University of Applied Sciences
description: Applied sciences university in the Rhine-Neckar tech corridor. Located steps away from SAP and surrounded by chemical and pharmaceutical giants. Strong engineering and CS programs with intensive industry cooperation. Good alternative to Mannheim University for applied tech.

name: Hochschule Augsburg
name_german: Hochschule Augsburg – University of Applied Sciences
description: Bavaria's applied sciences university in Augsburg, complementing the nearby Universität Augsburg. Strong in mechanical engineering, CS, and robotics thanks to KUKA Robotics' local presence. Good for students wanting practical tech education in Bavaria at lower cost than Munich.

name: Technische Hochschule Ingolstadt
description: Applied sciences university in Audi's hometown Ingolstadt. Unique MSc in Autonomous Driving Systems — one of the few dedicated programs globally. Deep partnership with Audi AG and its spin-offs. Perfect for CS/AI students passionate about automotive tech and self-driving vehicles.

name: Technische Hochschule Nürnberg Georg Simon Ohm
name_german: Technische Hochschule Nürnberg Georg Simon Ohm
description: Bavaria's largest applied sciences university named after physicist Georg Simon Ohm. Located in Nuremberg, one of Bavaria's major tech cities. Strong in automation, robotics, and electrical engineering. Siemens' global headquarters is in Nuremberg.

name: Hochschule für Technik Stuttgart
description: Specialized technical university of applied sciences in Stuttgart focused on computer science, geomatics, and infrastructure. Unique in Germany for its depth in photogrammetry and geoinformation. Located in Stuttgart's tech hub with excellent employer access.

name: Hochschule Koblenz
description: Applied sciences university at the confluence of Rhine and Moselle rivers. Campuses in Koblenz and Remagen. Good CS and engineering programs with affordable small-city living. Easy access to Frankfurt and Cologne via rail.

name: Hochschule Bremen
name_german: Hochschule Bremen – City University of Applied Sciences
description: Applied sciences university in the port city of Bremen. Strong aerospace connections via Airbus Bremen. The 'International Studies in Engineering' MSc is taught entirely in English and designed for international students. Space technology programs leverage OHB Systems proximity.

name: Hochschule RheinMain
name_german: Hochschule RheinMain – University of Applied Sciences
description: Applied sciences university in the Rhine-Main region with campuses in Wiesbaden and Rüsselsheim. Excellent proximity to Frankfurt Airport and banking sector. Strong applied informatics and engineering programs. Good gateway to the Frankfurt tech and finance ecosystem.

name: Jade Hochschule
description: Applied sciences university in northern Germany's coast with unique strengths in offshore engineering, geomatics, and maritime technology. Very affordable living costs. Growing wind energy and offshore tech sector offers interesting career paths for engineering students.
